47.text: to whom no warner had been sent that perhaps they might take heed, and that, on experiencing afflictions because of their own deeds, they may not say, "Lord, would that You had sent to us a Messenger so that we could follow Your revelations and become believers".

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And were it not that there should befall them a disaster for what their hands have sent before, then they should say: Our Lord! why didst Thou not send to us an apostle so that we should have followed Thy communications and been of the believers!

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: and may not say, if an affliction should befall them on account of their misdeeds: "Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger, we might have followed Your message and become believers."

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: If (We had) not (sent thee to the Quraish),- in case a calamity should seize them for (the deeds) that their hands have sent forth, they might say: "Our Lord! why didst Thou not sent us a messenger? We should then have followed Thy Signs and been amongst those who believe!"

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Walawla an tuseebahum museebatun bima qaddamat aydeehim fayaqooloo rabbana lawla arsalta ilayna rasoolan fanattabiAAa ayatika wanakoona mina almumineena

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said: "Why is he not given the like of what was given to Moses?" Did they not disbelieve before in what was given to Moses? They said: "Both are imposters one like the other," and added: "We do not believe in either."

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: So when the Truth* from Us came to them, they said, “Why has he not been given what was given to Moosa?” Had they not disbelieved in what was earlier given to Moosa? They said, “They are two magicians, who support each other”; and said, “We disbelieve in both of them.” (* Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him).

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Yet when the truth came to them 'from Ourselves, they said, 'Why has he not been given the like' of that Moses was given?' But they, did they not disbelieve also in what Moses was given aforetime? They said, 'A pair of sorceries mutually supporting each other.' They said, 'We disbelieve both.'

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: Yet when the truth is come unto them from before Us, they say: wherefore hast he not been vouchsafed the like of that which was given unto Musa? Disbelieved they not in that which was given unto Musa aforetime? They say; two magics supporting each other. And they say: verily in all such things we are disbelievers.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: But when the truth (i.e. Muhammad SAW with his Message) has come to them from Us, they say: "Why is he not given the like of what was given to Musa (Moses)? Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Musa (Moses) of old? They say: "Two kinds of magic [the Taurat (Torah) and the Quran] each helping the other!" And they say: "Verily! In both we are disbelievers."

48.author: Itani
48.text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, “If only he was given the like of what was given to Moses.” Did they not disbelieve in what was given to Moses in the past? They said, “Two works of magic backing one another.” And they said, “We are disbelieving in both.”

48.author: Maududi
48.text: But when the Truth reached them from Us, they said: "Why was he not given that which was given to Moses?" But did they not reject before what had been given to Moses? They said: "Both are magic, each supporting the other!" And they said: "We deny each of these."

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: But when the truth has come to them from Us, they say: "Why is he not given the like of what was given to Musa Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Musa of old" They say: "Two kinds of magic, each helping the other!" And they say: "Verily, in both we are disbelievers."

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: But when there came unto them the Truth from Our presence, they said: Why is he not given the like of what was given unto Moses? Did they not disbelieve in that which was given unto Moses of old? They say: Two magics that support each other; and they say: Lo! in both we are disbelievers.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: But when there came to them the truth from Us, they said, ‘Why has he not been given the like of what Moses was given?’ Did they not disbelieve what Moses was given before, and said, ‘Two magicians abetting each other,’ and said, ‘Indeed we disbelieve both of them’?

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: So when the truth (Prophet Muhammad) came to them from Us, they said: 'Why is he not given the like of that which was given to Moses' But did they not also disbelieve in that which Moses was given before! They say (about Moses and Aaron): 'Two sorcerers supporting one another' And they said: 'We disbelieve both'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, "Why was he not given like that which was given to Moses?" Did they not disbelieve in that which was given to Moses before? They said, "[They are but] two works of magic supporting each other, and indeed we are, in both, disbelievers."

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: When the Truth from Us came to them they said, "Would that he, (Muhammad), had received what was given to Moses (by his Lord)." Did not they reject what Moses had brought to them saying, "These two, Moses and Aaron, are two magicians who support each other. We do not have any faith in them."

48.author: Shakir
48.text: But (now) when the truth has come to them from Us, they say: Why is he not given the like of what was given to Musa? What! did they not disbelieve in what Musa was given before? They say: Two magicians backing up each other; and they say: Surely we are unbelievers in all.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: But when the truth came to them from Us, they said, "Why has he not been given the like of what Moses was given?" But did they not reject what Moses was given before? They said, "Both [Moses and Muhammad] are kinds of sorcery, each assisting the other." And they add, "We reject both of them."

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: But (now), when the Truth has come to them from Ourselves, they say, "Why are not (Signs) sent to him, like those which were sent to Moses?" Do they not then reject (the Signs) which were formerly sent to Moses? They say: "Two kinds of sorcery, each assisting the other!" And they say: "For us, we reject all (such things)!"

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Falamma jaahumu alhaqqu min AAindina qaloo lawla ootiya mithla ma ootiya moosa awalam yakfuroo bima ootiya moosa min qablu qaloo sihrani tathahara waqaloo inna bikullin kafiroona

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Say: "Then bring a Book from God which gives better guidance than these so that I may follow it, if you speak the truth."

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Therefore bring a Book from Allah which is better guiding than these two – I will follow it – if you are truthful.”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Say: 'Bring a Book from God that gives better guidance than these, and follow it, if you speak truly.'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Say thou: then bring a Book from before Allah, that is better in guidance than these two shall follow it--if ye are truth- tellers.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Say (to them, O Muhammad SAW): "Then bring a Book from Allah, which is a better guide than these two [the Taurat (Torah) and the Quran], that I may follow it, if you are truthful."

49.author: Itani
49.text: Say, “Then bring a scripture from God, more conductive to guidance than both, and I will follow it, if you are truthful.”

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): "Then do bring a Book from Allah which is a better guide than either of them, and I will follow it! Do so if you are truthful!"

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Say: "Then bring a Book from Allah, which is a better guide than these two, that I may follow it, if you are truthful."

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): Then bring a scripture from the presence of Allah that giveth clearer guidance than these two (that) I may follow it, if ye are truthful.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Say, ‘If you are truthful, bring some Book from Allah better in guidance than the two on them so that I may follow it.’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: Say: 'If what you say is true, bring down from Allah a Book that is a better guidance than both, I will follow it'

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Say, "Then bring a scripture from Allah which is more guiding than either of them that I may follow it, if you should be truthful."

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "Bring a Book if you are able to, from God better in its guidance than the Torah and the Quran; I shall follow it".

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Say: Then bring some (other) book from Allah which is a better guide than both of them, (that) I may follow it, if you are truthful.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Say to them, "Bring down from God a scripture that is a better guide than these two and I will follow it, if what you say be true."

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Say: "Then bring ye a Book from Allah, which is a better guide than either of them, that I may follow it! (do), if ye are truthful!"

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Qul fatoo bikitabin min AAindi Allahi huwa ahda minhuma attabiAAhu in kuntum sadiqeena

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: Then, if they cannot give you an answer, know that they are only following their lusts. And who can be farther astray than he who follows his lust without any guidance from God? And certainly God does not guide an unjust people.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: So if they do not accept your challenge, then know that they only follow their desires; and who is more astray than one who follows his desires away from the guidance of Allah? Indeed Allah does not guide the unjust.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Then if they do not answer thee, know that they are only following their caprices; and who is further astray than he who follows his caprice without guidance from God? Surely God guides not the people of the evildoers.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: Then if they answer thee not, know thou that they only follow their own desires; and who is farther astray than he who followeth his desire without guidance from Allah? Verily Allah guideth not a wrong- doing people.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: But if they answer you not (i.e. do not believe in your doctrine of Islamic Monotheism, nor follow you), then know that they only follow their own lusts. And who is more astray than one who follows his own lusts, without guidance from Allah? Verily! Allah guides not the people who are Zalimun (wrong-doers, disobedient to Allah, and polytheists).

50.author: Itani
50.text: But if they fail to respond to you, know that they follow their fancies. And who is more lost than him who follows his fancy without guidance from God? God does not guide the unjust people.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: But if they do not hearken to this, know well that they only follow their lusts and who is in greater error than he who follows his lusts without any guidance from Allah? Allah does not guide those given to wrong-doing.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: But if they answer you not, then know that they only follow their own lusts. And who is more astray than one who follows his own lusts, without guidance from Allah Verily, Allah guides not the people who are wrongdoers.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And if they answer thee not, then know that what they follow is their lusts. And who goeth farther astray than he who followeth his lust without guidance from Allah. Lo! Allah guideth not wrongdoing folk.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: Then if they do not respond to you[r] [summons], know that they only follow their desires, and who is more astray than him who follows his desires without any guidance from Allah? Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: If they do not answer you, know that they are only following their desires. And who is further astray than he who is led by his desires without guidance fromAllah! Allah does not guide the harmdoers.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: But if they do not respond to you - then know that they only follow their [own] desires. And who is more astray than one who follows his desire without guidance from Allah? Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: If they cannot meet such a challenge, know that they are only following their (evil) desires. Who strays more than one who follows his desires without guidance from God? God does not guide the unjust people.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: But if they do not answer you, then know that they only follow their low desires; and who is more erring than he who follows his low desires without any guidance from Allah? Surely Allah does not guide the unjust people.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: If they do not respond to you, then know that they follow only their own desires. Who could be more astray than he who follows his own likes and dislikes with no guidance from God? God does not guide the evil-doers.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: But if they hearken not to thee, know that they only follow their own lusts: and who is more astray than one who follow his own lusts, devoid of guidance from Allah? for Allah guides not people given to wrong-doing.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Fain lam yastajeeboo laka faiAAlam annama yattabiAAoona ahwaahum waman adallu mimmani ittabaAAa hawahu bighayri hudan mina Allahi inna Allaha la yahdee alqawma alththalimeena

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: We have been sending word to them that they may take a warning.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And We indeed sent down the Word continuously for them, so that they may ponder.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: Now We have brought them the Word; haply they may remember.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And assuredly We have caused the Word to reach them in succession, that haply they may be admonished.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And indeed now We have conveyed the Word (this Quran in which is the news of everything to them), in order that they may remember (or receive admonition).

51.author: Itani
51.text: We have delivered the Word to them, that they may remember.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: We have constantly conveyed them the word (of admonition) that they may take heed.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And indeed now We have conveyed the Word to them, in order that they may remember.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And now verily We have caused the Word to reach them, that haply they may give heed.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Certainly We have carried on this discourse for them so that they may take admonition.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: We have brought them the Word in order that they remember.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And We have [repeatedly] conveyed to them the Qur'an that they might be reminded.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: We sent Our guidance to them so that perhaps they might take heed.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And certainly We have made the word to reach them so that they may be mindful.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: We have conveyed Our Word to them, in succession, so that they may give heed.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Now have We caused the Word to reach them themselves, in order that they may receive admonition.

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Walaqad wassalna lahumu alqawla laAAallahum yatathakkaroona

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before this do believe in it;

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before it, accept faith in it. (Some scholars among Jews / Christians).

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before this believe in it

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Those unto whom We vouchsafed the Book before it, --they believe therein.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture [i.e. the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel), etc.] before it, - they believe in it (the Quran).

52.author: Itani
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it believe in it.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: Those on whom We bestowed the Book before do believe in this (to wit, the Qur'an).

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it, they believe in it.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: Those unto whom We gave the Scripture before it, they believe in it,

52.author: Qarai
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before it are the ones who believe in it,

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before, believe in it.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture before it - they are believers in it.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: (Some of) the followers of the Bible believe in the Quran.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: (As to) those whom We gave the Book before it, they are believers in it.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Those to whom We gave the Book before this believe in it [the Quran],

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Those to whom We sent the Book before this,- they do believe in this (revelation):

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Allatheena ataynahumu alkitaba min qablihi hum bihi yuminoona

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: And when it is read out to them, say: "We believe in it. It's the truth from our Lord. We had committed ourselves before it came."

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And when these verses are recited to them, they say, “We accept faith in it – indeed it is the Truth from our Lord – and indeed we had surrendered even before this.”

53.author: Arberry
53.text: and, when it is recited to them, they say, 'We believe in it; surely it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed, even before' it we had surrendered.'

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And when it is rehearsed unto them they say: we believe therein: verily it is truth from our Lord: verily we have been even before it of those who submit themselves.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And when it is recited to them, they say: "We believe in it. Verily, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed even before it we have been from those who submit themselves to Allah in Islam as Muslims (like 'Abdullah bin Salam and Salman Al-Farisi, etc.).

53.author: Itani
53.text: When it is recited to them, they say, “We have believed in it; it is the truth from our Lord; we were Muslims prior to it.”

53.author: Maududi
53.text: When it is recited to them they say: "We believe in it for it is the Truth from our Lord. Indeed we were already Muslims."

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And when it is recited to them, they say: "We believe in it. Verily, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed even before it we have been from Muslims."

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And when it is recited unto them, they say: We believe in it. Lo! it is the Truth from our Lord. Lo! even before it we were of those who surrender (unto Him).

53.author: Qarai
53.text: and when it is recited to them, they say, ‘We believe in it. It is indeed the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were Muslims [even] before it [came].’

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: When it is recited to them, they say: 'We believe in it, because it is the truth from Our Lord. We surrendered ourselves before it came.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And when it is recited to them, they say, "We have believed in it; indeed, it is the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were, [even] before it, Muslims [submitting to Allah]."

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: When it is recited to them, they say, "We believe in it. It is the Truth from our Lord. We were Muslims before it was revealed".

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And when it is recited to them they say: We believe in it surely it is the truth from our Lord; surely we were submitters before this.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: and, when it is recited to them, they say, "We believe in it. Indeed it is the truth from our Lord. Even before it came, we had submitted ourselves."

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: And when it is recited to them, they say: "We believe therein, for it is the Truth from our Lord: indeed we have been Muslims (bowing to Allah's Will) from before this.

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Waitha yutla AAalayhim qaloo amanna bihi innahu alhaqqu min rabbina inna kunna min qablihi muslimeena

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: These will be given their recompense twice, for they persevered and repelled evil with good, and spent of what We had given them.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: They will be given double the reward, the recompense of their patience – and they repel evil with good, and spend part of what We have provided them.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: These shall be given their wage twice over for that they patiently endured, and avert evil with good, and expend of that We have provided them.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: These shall be vouchsafed their hire twice over, because they have persevered, and they repel evil with good, and expend of that wherewith We have provided them.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: These will be given their reward twice over, because they are patient, and repel evil with good, and spend (in charity) out of what We have provided them.

54.author: Itani
54.text: These will be given their reward twice, because they persevered; and they counter evil with good; and from Our provisions to them, they give.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: These will be granted their reward twice over because they remained steadfast; they repel evil with good, and spend (in alms) out of the sustenance We provided them,

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: These will be given their reward twice over, because they are patient, and repel evil with good, and spend out of what We have provided for them.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: These will be given their reward twice over, because they are steadfast and repel evil with good, and spend of that wherewith We have provided them,

54.author: Qarai
54.text: Those will be given their reward two times for their patience. They repel evil [conduct] with good, and spend out of what We have provided them,

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: 'Their wage shall be given them twice, because they have endured patiently, averting evil with good and spending from what We have given them;

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: Those will be given their reward twice for what they patiently endured and [because] they avert evil through good, and from what We have provided them they spend.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: These will receive double reward for their forbearance, replacing evil by virtue, and for their spending for the cause of God.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: These shall be granted their reward twice, because they are steadfast and they repel evil with good and spend out of what We have given them.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: Such people as these will receive a double reward, because they are steadfast and repel evil with good, and give alms out of what We have given them,

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: Twice will they be given their reward, for that they have persevered, that they avert Evil with Good, and that they spend (in charity) out of what We have given them.

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Olaika yutawna ajrahum marratayni bima sabaroo wayadraoona bialhasanati alssayyiata wamimma razaqnahum yunfiqoona

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: When they hear idle talk they turn aside and say: "To us our actions, to you yours. Peace on you; we do not look for the ignorant."

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: And when they hear indecent speech, they ignore it and say, “For us are our deeds and for you are your deeds; peace be to you (good bye); we are not interested in the ignorant.”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: When they hear idle talk, they turn away from it and say, 'We have our deeds, and you your deeds. Peace be upon your We desire not the ignorant.'

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And when they hear vain discourse they withdraw therefrom and say: unto US our works, and unto you your works; peace be unto you; we seek not the ignorant.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: And when they hear Al-Laghw (dirty, false, evil vain talk), they withdraw from it and say: "To us our deeds, and to you your deeds. Peace be to you. We seek not the ignorant."

55.author: Itani
55.text: And when they hear vain talk, they avoid it, and say, “We have our deeds, and you have your deeds; peace be upon you; we do not desire the ignorant.”

55.author: Maududi
55.text: and when they hear any vain talk, they turn away from it, saying: "We have our deeds and you have your deeds. Peace be to you. We do not desire to act like the ignorant."

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: And when they hear evil vain talk, they withdraw from it and say: "To us our deeds, and to you your deeds. Peace be to you. We seek not (the way of) the ignorant."

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: And when they hear vanity they withdraw from it and say: Unto us our works and unto you your works. Peace be unto you! We desire not the ignorant.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: and when they hear vain talk, they avoid it and say, ‘Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. Peace be to you. We do not court the ignorant.’

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: and because when they hear idle talk they turn away from it and say: 'We have our deeds, and you have your deeds. Peace be upon you. We do not desire the ignorant'

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: And when they hear ill speech, they turn away from it and say, "For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds. Peace will be upon you; we seek not the ignorant."

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: When they hear impious words, they ignore them, saying, "We shall be responsible for our deeds and you will be responsible for yours. Peace be with you. We do not want to become ignorant."

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And when they hear idle talk they turn aside from it and say: We shall have our deeds and you shall have your deeds; peace be on you, we do not desire the ignorant.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: and when they hear vain talk, they turn away from it and say, "We have our actions and you have yours. We wish you peace. We will have nothing to do with the ignorant."

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: And when they hear vain talk, they turn away therefrom and say: "To us our deeds, and to you yours; peace be to you: we seek not the ignorant."

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Waitha samiAAoo allaghwa aAAradoo AAanhu waqaloo lana aAAmaluna walakum aAAmalukum salamun AAalaykum la nabtaghee aljahileena

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: You cannot guide any one you like: God guides whosoever He please. He knows best who will come to guidance.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: Indeed it is not such that you can guide whomever you love, but Allah guides whomever He wills; and He well knows the people upon guidance.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: Thou guidest not whom thou likest, but God guides whom He wills, and knows very well those that are guided.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: Verily thou shalt not guide whomsoever thou lovest, but Allah shall guide whomsoever He will. And He knoweth best who are the guided.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: Verily! You (O Muhammad SAW) guide not whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are the guided.

56.author: Itani
56.text: You cannot guide whom you love, but God guides whom He wills, and He knows best those who are guided.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: (O Prophet), you cannot grant guidance to whom you please. It is Allah Who guides those whom He will. He knows best who are amenable to guidance.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: Verily, you guide not whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are the guided.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: Lo! thou (O Muhammad) guidest not whom thou lovest, but Allah guideth whom He will. And He is Best Aware of those who walk aright.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: You cannot guide whomever you wish, but [it is] Allah [who] guides whomever He wishes, and He knows best those who are guided.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: You cannot guide whom you please; it is Allah who guides whom He will. He knows well those who are guided.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: Indeed, [O Muhammad], you do not guide whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: (Muhammad), you cannot guide whomever you love, but God guides whomever He wants and knows best those who seek guidance.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: Surely you cannot guide whom you love, but Allah guides whom He pleases, and He knows best the followers of the right way.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: You cannot guide whoever you please: it is God who guides whom He will. He best knows those who would accept guidance.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: It is true thou wilt not be able to guide every one, whom thou lovest; but Allah guides those whom He will and He knows best those who receive guidance.

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Innaka la tahdee man ahbabta walakinna Allaha yahdee man yashao wahuwa aAAlamu bialmuhtadeena

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: They say: "If we followed your guidance we would be driven from the land by force." Have We not set up a sanctuary for them, to which is brought a wealth of everything as provision from Us? But most of them do not understand.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: And they say, “If we follow the guidance along with you, people would snatch us away from our land”; did We not establish them in a safe Sacred Land, towards which are brought fruits of all kinds – the sustenance from Us? But most of them do not know.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: They say, 'Should we follow the guidance with thee, we shall be snatched from our land.' Have We not established for them a sanctuary secure, to which are collected the fruits of everything, as a provision from Us? But most of them know not.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: And they say: were we to follow the guidance with thee, We Shall be snatched away from our land. Have We not established for them an inviolable sanctuary whereunto fruits of every sort are brought: a provision from Our presence? But most of them know not.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: And they say: "If we follow the guidance with you, we would be snatched away from our land." Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary (Makkah), to which are brought fruits of all kinds, a provision from Ourselves, but most of them know not.

57.author: Itani
57.text: And they say, “If we follow the guidance with you, we will be snatched from our land.” Did We not establish for them a Safe Sanctuary, to which are brought all kinds of fruits, as provision from Ourselves? But most of them do not know.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: They say: "If we were to follow this guidance with you, we should be snatched away from our land." Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary to which fruits of all kinds are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: And they say: "If we follow the guidance with you, we would be snatched away from our land." Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary, to which are brought fruits of all kinds, a provision from Ourselves, but most of them know not.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: And they say: If we were to follow the Guidance with thee we should be torn out of our land. Have We not established for them a sure sanctuary, whereunto the produce of all things is brought (in trade), a provision from Our presence? But most of them know not.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: They say, ‘If we follow the guidance with you, we will be forced out of our territory.’ Did We not establish a secure sanctuary for them where fruits of all kinds are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: They say: 'If we follow the guidance with you, we shall be driven from our land' But have We not given them a secure sanctuary from which fruits of every kind are collected as a provision from Us? Indeed, most of them do not know.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: And they say, "If we were to follow the guidance with you, we would be swept from our land." Have we not established for them a safe sanctuary to which are brought the fruits of all things as provision from Us? But most of them do not know.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: They, (the pagans), say, "If we were to follow your guidance we would be snatched away from our land. Have We not given them the secure, holy precinct wherein all types of fruits are brought to them as a sustenance from Us? However, many of them do not know it.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: And they say: If we follow the guidance with you, we shall be carried off from our country. What! have We not settled them in a safe, sacred territory to which fruits of every kind shall be drawn?-- a sustenance from Us; but most of them do not know.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: They say, "If we were to follow your guidance, we should be uprooted from our land." But have We not established for them a safe haven to which fruits of every kind are brought as a provision from Ourself? But most of them have no knowledge.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: They say: "If we were to follow the guidance with thee, we should be snatched away from our land." Have We not established for them a secure sanctuary, to which are brought as tribute fruits of all kinds,- a provision from Ourselves? but most of them understand not.

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Waqaloo in nattabiAAi alhuda maAAaka nutakhattaf min ardina awalam numakkin lahum haraman aminan yujba ilayhi thamaratu kulli shayin rizqan min ladunna walakinna aktharahum la yaAAlamoona

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: How many habitations that had come to boast of their resources have We destroyed? These their dwellings were never inhabited except rarely after them; and they came back to Us.

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And how many towns did We destroy which had stooped to self indulgence! So here lie their dwellings, uninhabited after them except a little; and only We are the Inheritors.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: How many a city We have destroyed that flourished in insolent ease! Those are their dwelling-places, undwelt in after them, except a little; Ourselves are the inheritors.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And how many a city have We destroyed that exulted in their living! And yonder are their dwellings which have not been inhabited after them unless for a little while; and verily We! We have been the inheritors.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And how many a town (population) have We destroyed, which were thankless for its means of livelihood (disobeyed Allah, and His Messengers, by doing evil deeds and crimes)! And those are their dwellings, which have not been inhabited after them except a little. And verily! We have been the inheritors.

58.author: Itani
58.text: And how many a city did We destroy for turning unappreciative of its livelihood? Here are their homes, uninhabited after them, except for a few. And We became the Inheritors.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: And how many a town did We destroy whose inhabitants exulted on account of their affluence. These are their dwellings in which very few dwelt after them. Eventually it is We Who inherited them.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And how many a town have We destroyed, which was thankless for its means of livelihood! And those are their dwellings, which have not been inhabited after them except a little. And verily, We have been the heirs.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And how many a community have We destroyed that was thankless for its means of livelihood! And yonder are their dwellings, which have not been inhabited after them save a little. And We, even We, were the inheritors.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: How many a town We have destroyed that exulted in its lifestyle! There lie their dwellings, uninhabited after them except by a few, and We were the [sole] inheritors.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: How many a village have We destroyed that were ungrateful in their life! Those are their dwellings, (they are) uninhabited after them except for a little; We are the inheritors.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And how many a city have We destroyed that was insolent in its [way of] living, and those are their dwellings which have not been inhabited after them except briefly. And it is We who were the inheritors.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: How many nations, who had enjoyed great prosperity, had We destroyed? Those are their homes which were not inhabited thereafter except for a short time. Only We were their heirs.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And how many a town have We destroyed which exulted in its means of subsistence, so these are their abodes, they have not been dwelt in after them except a little, and We are the inheritors,

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: How many townships have We destroyed where the people had become arrogant on account of their affluence? Since then their dwelling-places have scarcely been inhabited -- We became their inheritors.

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: And how many populations We destroyed, which exulted in their life (of ease and plenty)! now those habitations of theirs, after them, are deserted,- All but a (miserable) few! and We are their heirs!

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Wakam ahlakna min qaryatin batirat maAAeeshataha fatilka masakinuhum lam tuskan min baAAdihim illa qaleelan wakunna nahnu alwaritheena

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: But your Lord does not destroy habitations without having sent an apostle to their metropolis to read out Our commandments to them. We would never have destroyed cities if their inhabitants were not given to wickedness.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: And never does your Lord destroy towns until He sends a Noble Messenger to their principal town, reciting Our verses to them; and We never destroy towns unless its people are unjust.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: Yet thy Lord never destroyed the cities until He sent in their mother-city a Messenger, to recite Our signs unto them; and We never destroyed the cities, save that their inhabitants were evildoers.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Nor was thy Lord to destroy the Cities, until He had raised up in their mother-city an apostle reciting unto them our revealations. Nor were We to destroy the cities unless the inhabitants thereof were wrong-doers.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: And never will your Lord destroy the towns (populations) until He sends to their mother town a Messenger reciting to them Our Verses. And never would We destroy the towns unless the people thereof are Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah, oppressors and tyrants).

59.author: Itani
59.text: Your Lord never destroys cities without first sending a messenger in their midst, reciting to them Our revelations. And We never destroy the cities, unless their people are wrongdoers.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: Your Lord would not destroy a town until He had sent to its centre a Messenger who would recite to them Our verses. Nor would We destroy any town unless its inhabitants were iniquitous.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: And never will your Lord destroy the towns until He sends to their mother town a Messenger reciting to them Our Ayat. And never would We destroy the towns unless the people thereof are wrongdoers.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: And never did thy Lord destroy the townships, till He had raised up in their mother(-town) a messenger reciting unto them Our revelations. And never did We destroy the townships unless the folk thereof were evil-doers.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: Your Lord would not destroy the towns until He had raised an apostle in their mother city to recite to them Our signs. We would never destroy the towns except when their people were wrongdoers.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: Nor did your Lord destroy the villages until He had sent a Messenger to its mother village reciting to them Our verses. We never destroyed villages unless their inhabitants were harmdoers.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: And never would your Lord have destroyed the cities until He had sent to their mother a messenger reciting to them Our verses. And We would not destroy the cities except while their people were wrongdoers.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: Your Lord did not destroy the people of the towns without first sending a Messenger to the mother town who would recite His revelations to them. We did not want to destroy the towns if the people therein were not unjust.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: And your Lord never destroyed the towns until He raised in their metropolis an apostle, reciting to them Our communications, and We never destroyed the towns except when their people were unjust.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: Your Lord would never destroy a people until He had sent messengers to their capital cities, reciting to them Our revelations. Nor did We destroy a town unless their people became wrongdoers.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Nor was thy Lord the one to destroy a population until He had sent to its centre a messenger, rehearsing to them Our Signs; nor are We going to destroy a population except when its members practise iniquity.

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Wama kana rabbuka muhlika alqura hatta yabAAatha fee ommiha rasoolan yatloo AAalayhim ayatina wama kunna muhlikee alqura illa waahluha thalimoona

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: Whatsoever has been given you is the stuff this life is made of, and only its embellishment. What is with your Lord is better and abiding. Will you not understand?

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: And whatever you have been given, is a usage in the life of this world and its adornment; and that which is with Allah is better and more lasting; so do you not have sense?

60.author: Arberry
60.text: Whatever thing you have been given is the enjoyment of the present life and its adornment; and what is with God is better and more enduring.

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: And whatsoever ye are vouchsafed is an enjoyment of the life of the world and an adornment thereof; and that which is with Allah is better and more lasting. Will ye not therefore reflect?

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: And whatever you have been given is an enjoyment of the life of (this) world and its adornment, and that (Hereafter) which is with Allah is better and will remain forever. Have you then no sense?

60.author: Itani
60.text: Whatever thing you are given is but the material of this world, and its glitter. But what is with God is better, and longer lasting. Do you not comprehend?

60.author: Maududi
60.text: Whatever you have been given is a provision for the life of this world and its glitter. But that which is with Allah is better and more enduring. Do you not use your intellect?

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: And whatever you have been given is an enjoyment of the life of the world and its adornment, and that which is with Allah is better and will remain forever. Have you then no sense

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: And whatsoever ye have been given is a comfort of the life of the world and an ornament thereof; and that which Allah hath is better and more lasting. Have ye then no sense?

60.author: Qarai
60.text: Whatever things you have been given are only the wares of the life of this world and its glitter, and what is with Allah is better and more lasting. Will you not exercise your reason?

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: The things you have been given, and its adornments, are but the enjoyment of this present life. What is with Allah is better and everlasting. Do you not understand?

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: And whatever thing you [people] have been given - it is [only for] the enjoyment of worldly life and its adornment. And what is with Allah is better and more lasting; so will you not use reason?

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Whatever you (people) have been given are only the means for enjoyment and beauty of the worldly life, but the means of enjoyment (which you will receive from God) in the life to come will be better and everlasting. Will you then not take heed?

60.author: Shakir
60.text: And whatever things you have been given are only a provision of this world's life and its adornment, and whatever is with Allah is better and more lasting; do you not then understand?

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Whatever you are given in this life is nothing but a temporary provision of this life and its glitter; what God has is better and more lasting. Will you not then understand?

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: The (material) things which ye are given are but the conveniences of this life and the glitter thereof; but that which is with Allah is better and more enduring: will ye not then be wise?

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Wama ooteetum min shayin famataAAu alhayati alddunya wazeenatuha wama AAinda Allahi khayrun waabqa afala taAAqiloona

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: Can one who was given a better promise, which he will find come true, be like him who was given a little enjoyment of this life, but who will afterwards be brought (to judgement) on the Day of Resurrection?

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: So is he whom We have given a good promise – he will therefore get it – equal to him whom We gave the usage of the life of this world to enjoy, and who will then be brought captive on the Day of Resurrection?

61.author: Arberry
61.text: Will you not understand? What, is he to whom We have promised a fair promise, and he receives it, like him to whom We have given the enjoyment of the present life, then he on the Resurrection Day shall be of those that are arraigned?

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Is he, then, whom We have promised an excellent promise which he is going to meet, like unto him whom We have suffered to enjoy awhile the enjoyment of the life of the world, then on the Day of Resurrection he shall be of those brought up!

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: Is he whom We have promised an excellent promise (Paradise), which he will find true, like him whom We have made to enjoy the luxuries of the life of (this) world, then on the Day of Resurrection, he will be among those brought up (to be punished in the Hell-fire)?

61.author: Itani
61.text: Can someone to whom We have made a fine promise—which he will attain—be equal to someone to whom We have given enjoyments in this world, but who will be, on Resurrection Day, among the arraigned?

61.author: Maududi
61.text: Now, he to whom We have promised a good which he is going to obtain - can he be like him whom We have given the good things of this life, but who will be brought up for punishment on the Day of Judgement?

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: Is he whom We have promised an excellent promise -- which he will find true -- like him whom We have made to enjoy the luxuries of the life of the world, then on the Day of Resurrection, he will be among those brought up

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Is he whom We have promised a fair promise which he will find (true) like him whom We suffer to enjoy awhile the comfort of the life of the world, then on the Day of Resurrection he will be of those arraigned?

61.author: Qarai
61.text: Is he to whom We have given a good promise, which he will receive, like him whom We have provided the wares of the life of this world, but who will be arraigned on the Day of Resurrection?

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: Is he to whom We promised a fine promise and receives it, like he who has been given the enjoyment of this present life, then on the Day of Resurrection shall be among those that are arraigned?

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: Then is he whom We have promised a good promise which he will obtain like he for whom We provided enjoyment of worldly life [but] then he is, on the Day of Resurrection, among those presented [for punishment in Hell]?

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: Is the case of those to whom We have promised good things - which they will certainly receive in the life to come - equal to the case of those to whom We have granted the means of enjoyment in the worldly life and who will certainly be questioned about them in the life to come?

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Is he to whom We have promised a goodly promise which he shall meet with like him whom We have provided with the provisions of this world's life, then on the day of resurrection he shall be of those who are brought up?

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: Can someone to whom We have made a gracious promise and who will see it fulfilled, be compared to someone We have allowed to enjoy a worldly life, awhile, and who will be brought up [before God] for his accounting on the Day of Resurrection?

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: Are (these two) alike?- one to whom We have made a goodly promise, and who is going to reach its (fulfilment), and one to whom We have given the good things of this life, but who, on the Day of Judgment, is to be among those brought up (for punishment)?

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Afaman waAAadnahu waAAdan hasanan fahuwa laqeehi kaman mattaAAnahu mataAAa alhayati alddunya thumma huwa yawma alqiyamati mina almuhdareena

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: That day God will call them and ask: "Where are they you imagined were My compeers?"

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: On the day when He will call to them – He will therefore proclaim, “Where are those partners of Mine, whom you had assumed?”

62.author: Arberry
62.text: Upon the day when He shall call to them, and He shall say, 'Where now are My associates whom you were asserting?'

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: And on the Day whereon He shall call unto them and say: where are My associates whom ye were wont to assert?

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: And (remember) the Day when He will call to them, and say: "Where are My (so-called) partners whom you used to assert?"

62.author: Itani
62.text: On the Day when He will call to them, and say, “Where are My associates whom you used to claim?”

62.author: Maududi
62.text: (Let them not forget) that the Day when Allah will call unto them, and say: "Where are those whom you imagined to be My associates?"

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: And (remember) the Day when He will call to them and say: "Where are My (so-called) partners whom you used to assert"

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: On the day when He will call unto them and say: Where are My partners whom ye imagined?

62.author: Qarai
62.text: The day He will call out to them and ask, ‘Where are My ‘partners’ that you used to claim?’

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: On that Day He will call to them, saying: 'Where are those whom you alleged to be My associates'

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: And [warn of] the Day He will call them and say, "Where are My 'partners' which you used to claim?"

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: On the day when He will ask (the latter group), "Where are those whom you had considered equal to Me?"

62.author: Shakir
62.text: And on the day when He will call them and say: Where are those whom you deemed to be My associates?

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: On that Day He will call to them, and say, "Where are those whom you claimed to be My partners?"

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: That Day (Allah) will call to them, and say "Where are my 'partners'?- whom ye imagined (to be such)?"

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Wayawma yunadeehim fayaqoolu ayna shurakaiya allatheena kuntum tazAAumoona

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Those against whom the sentence is justified will say: "O Lord, these are those we had led astray as we had ourselves gone astray. We clear ourselves before You: They never worshipped us."

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: Those against whom the Word will have proved true will say, “Our Lord! These are they whom we led astray; we led them astray the way we ourselves went astray; we are unconcerned with them and we incline towards You – they never worshipped us!”

63.author: Arberry
63.text: Those against whom the Word is realized they shall say, 'Our Lord, those whom we perverted, we perverted them even as we ourselves erred. We declare our innocence unto Thee; it was not us that they were serving.'

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Those upon whom the sentence will be pronounced will say: our Lord these are they whom we seduced, we seduced them even as we our selves were seduced. We declare ourselves quit of them before Thee; not our selves they were wont to worship.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Those about whom the Word will have come true (to be punished) will say: "Our Lord! These are they whom we led astray. We led them astray, as we were astray ourselves. We declare our innocence (from them) before You. It was not us they worshipped."

63.author: Itani
63.text: Those against whom the sentence is justified will say, “Our Lord, these are they whom we misled. We misled them, as we were misled. We beg Your forgiveness; it was not us they used to worship.”

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Those against whom the Word will be realised will say: "Our Lord, these are the ones whom we led astray just as we ourselves strayed. We absolve ourselves before You of all blame. It was not us that they worshipped."

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: Those about whom the Word will have been fulfilled will say: "Our Lord! These are they whom we led astray. We led them astray, as we were astray ourselves. We declare our innocence before You. It was not us they worshipped."

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Those concerning whom the Word will have come true will say: Our Lord! These are they whom we led astray. We led them astray even as we ourselves were astray. We declare our innocence before Thee: us they never worshipped.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: Those against whom the word had become due will say, ‘Our Lord! These are the ones whom we have perverted. We perverted them as we were perverse ourselves. We repudiate them before You: it was not us that they worshipped.’

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: Those against whom the Word is realized shall say: 'Our Lord, those whom we led astray, we led them astray even as we ourselves were astray. We are quit of them to You; it was not us that they worshipped'

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Those upon whom the word will have come into effect will say, "Our Lord, these are the ones we led to error. We led them to error just as we were in error. We declare our disassociation [from them] to You. They did not used to worship us."

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: Those who have become subject to punishment will say, "Lord, they seduced us." Their idols will say, "We seduced them but we renounce their worshipping us for it was not us whom they worshipped".

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Those against whom the sentence has become confirmed will say: Our Lord! these are they whom we caused to err; we caused them to err as we ourselves did err; to Thee we declare ourselves to be clear (of them); they never served Us.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: And those on whom sentence has been passed, will say, "Our Lord, these are the ones who led us astray. We led them astray as we ourselves were led astray. We now dissociate ourselves from them before You; it was not us that they worshipped."

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Those against whom the charge will be proved, will say: "Our Lord! These are the ones whom we led astray: we led them astray, as we were astray ourselves: we free ourselves (from them) in Thy presence: it was not us they worshipped."

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Qala allatheena haqqa AAalayhimu alqawlu rabbana haolai allatheena aghwayna aghwaynahum kama ghawayna tabarrana ilayka ma kanoo iyyana yaAAbudoona

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: (The sinners) will be told: "Invoke your partners." They will call on them, but they will not answer; and they will see the torment (and wish) if only they had come to guidance.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: And it will be said to them, “Call unto your ascribed partners” – so they will call unto them and they will not listen to them, and they will behold the punishment; if only they had obtained guidance!

64.author: Arberry
64.text: It shall be said, 'Call you now upon your associates!' And they will call upon them, but they shall not answer them, and they shall see the chastisement-ah, if they had been guided!

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: And it shall be said: Call upon your associate-gods. And they shall call upon them, and they shall not answer them, and they shall behold the torment. Would that they had received the guidance!

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: And it will be said (to them): "Call upon your (so-called) partners (of Allah), and they will call upon them, but they will give no answer to them, and they will see the torment. (They will then wish) if only they had been guided!

64.author: Itani
64.text: And it will be said, “Call on your partners.” And they will call on them, but they will not respond to them. And they will see the suffering. If only they were guided.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: They will then be told: "Call upon those for help whom you declared to be Our associates." They will then call upon them but they will not answer them. They will have observed the chastisement in front of them. Would that they were guided!

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: And it will be said: "Call upon your partners," and they will call upon them, but they will give no answer to them, and they will see the torment. If only they had been guided!

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: And it will be said: Cry unto your (so-called) partners (of Allah). And they will cry unto them, and they will give no answer unto them, and they will see the Doom. Ah, if they had but been guided!

64.author: Qarai
64.text: It will be said, ‘Invoke your partners!’ So they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them, and they will sight the punishment, wishing they had followed guidance.

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: It will be said to them: 'Call on your associates' And they will call on them, but they will not respond to them, and they shall see the punishment if only they had been guided.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: And it will be said, "Invoke your 'partners' " and they will invoke them; but they will not respond to them, and they will see the punishment. If only they had followed guidance!

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: They will be told to call their idols. They will call them but will receive no answer. They will see the torment approaching and wish that they had sought guidance.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: And it will be said: Call your associate-gods. So they will call upon them, but they will not answer them, and they shall see the punishment; would that they had followed the right way!

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: Then they will be told, "Call upon your partners." And they will call upon them, but will receive no answer. They shall witness the punishment. If only they had allowed themselves to be guided.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: It will be said (to them): "Call upon your 'partners' (for help)": they will call upon them, but they will not listen to them; and they will see the Penalty (before them); (how they will wish) 'if only they had been open to guidance!'

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Waqeela odAAoo shurakaakum fadaAAawhum falam yastajeeboo lahum waraawoo alAAathaba law annahum kanoo yahtadoona

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: (God) will ask them on that day: "What answer did you give the apostles?"

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: And on the Day when He will call to them and proclaim, “What answer did you give to the Noble Messengers?”

65.author: Arberry
65.text: Upon the day when He shall call to them, and He shall say, 'What answer gave you to the Envoys?'

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: And on the Day whereon He shall call unto them and say: what answer gave ye to the sent ones?

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: And (remember) the Day (Allah) will call to them, and say: "What answer gave you to the Messengers?"

65.author: Itani
65.text: On the Day when He will call to them, and say, “What did you answer the Messengers?”

65.author: Maududi
65.text: (Let them not disregard) that the Day when Allah will call out to them saying: "What was the answer you gave to the Messengers?"

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: And the Day He will call to them, and say: "What answer gave you to the Messengers"

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: And on the Day when He will call unto them and say: What answer gave ye to the messengers?

65.author: Qarai
65.text: The day He will call out to them and say, ‘What response did you give to the apostles?’

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: On that Day He will call to them and He will say: 'What response did you give Our Messengers'

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: And [mention] the Day He will call them and say, "What did you answer the messengers?"

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: On the day when God will call them and ask them, "What answer did you give to (Our) messengers?"

65.author: Shakir
65.text: And on the day when He shall call them and say: What was the answer you gave to the apostles?

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: On that Day God will call out to them, saying, "What answer did you give to Our messengers?"

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: That Day (Allah) will call to them, and say: "What was the answer ye gave to the messengers?"

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Wayawma yunadeehim fayaqoolu matha ajabtumu almursaleena

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: Then all news will be blacked out for them, and they will not even ask one another.

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: So on that Day the tidings will be blinded* for them, therefore they will not ask one another. (* They will forget at that moment).

66.author: Arberry
66.text: Upon that day the tidings will be darkened for them, nor will they ask each other.

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: Bedimmed unto them shall be all excuses on that Day, wherefore they shall not be able to ask one of another.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: Then the news of a good answer will be obscured to them on that day, and they will not be able to ask one another.

66.author: Itani
66.text: They will be blinded by the facts on that Day, and they will not question each other.

66.author: Maududi
66.text: Then they will not be able to think of any reply, nor will they be able to ask one another.

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: Then the news of a good answer will be obscured to them on that Day, and they will not be able to ask one another.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: On that day (all) tidings will be dimmed for them, nor will they ask one of another,

66.author: Qarai
66.text: That day all information will be withheld from them, so they will not question one another.

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: And on that Day the news will be blinded for them, and they will not ask each other.

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: But the information will be unapparent to them that Day, so they will not [be able to] ask one another.

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: The door to all answers will be closed to them and they will not even be able to ask one another.

66.author: Shakir
66.text: Then the pleas shall become obscure to them on that day, so they shall not ask each other.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: They will be left speechless on that Day, and they will not be able to consult each other.

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: Then the (whole) story that Day will seem obscure to them (like light to the blind) and they will not be able (even) to question each other.

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: FaAAamiyat AAalayhimu alanbao yawmaithin fahum la yatasaaloona

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: But he who repents and believes and does good things may well be among the successful.

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: So whoever repented and accepted faith and did good deeds, so it is likely that he will be among the successful.

67.author: Arberry
67.text: But as for him who repents, and believes, and works righteousness, haply he shall be among the prosperers.

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Howbeit, whosoever shall repent and believe, and work righteous works - belike he shall be of the thrivers.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: But as for him who repented (from polytheism and sins, etc.), believed (in the Oneness of Allah, and in His Messenger Muhammad SAW), and did righteous deeds (in the life of this world), then he will be among those who are successful.

67.author: Itani
67.text: But he who repents, and believes, and does righteous deeds, may well be among the winners.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: But those who repented and believed and acted righteously, they will perhaps be among those who will prosper there.

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: But as for him who repented, believed, and did righteous deeds, then perhaps he will be among those who are successful.

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: But as for him who shall repent and believe and do right, he haply may be one of the successful.

67.author: Qarai
67.text: As for him who repents, has faith and acts righteously, maybe he will be among the felicitous.

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: But, as for he who repents and believes, and does good deeds, he will be among those who prosper.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: But as for one who had repented, believed, and done righteousness, it is promised by Allah that he will be among the successful.

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: However, those who have repented and have become righteously striving believers will perhaps have everlasting happiness.

67.author: Shakir
67.text: But as to him who repents and believes and does good, maybe he will be among the successful:

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: But as for him who repents and believes and does good deeds, he can hope to find himself among the successful.

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: But any that (in this life) had repented, believed, and worked righteousness, will have hopes to be among those who achieve salvation.

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Faamma man taba waamana waAAamila salihan faAAasa an yakoona mina almufliheena

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: Your Lord creates what He wills and chooses. The good is not for them to choose. Too holy and high is God for what they associate with Him.

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: And your Lord creates whatever He wills, and chooses; they do not have any power to choose; Purity and Supremacy are to Allah above their ascribing partners (to Him).

68.author: Arberry
68.text: Thy Lord creates whatsoever He will and He chooses; they have not the choice. Glory be to God! High be He exalted above that they associate!

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: And thy lord createth whatsoever He listeth and chooseth; no Choice is to be far them. Hallowed be Allah and exalted above that which they associate!

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: And your Lord creates whatsoever He wills and chooses, no choice have they (in any matter). Glorified be Allah, and exalted above all that they associate as partners (with Him).

68.author: Itani
68.text: Your Lord creates whatever He wills, and He chooses. The choice is not theirs. Glory be to God, and exalted be He above the associations they make.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: Your Lord creates what He will and chooses (for His tasks) whomsoever He will. It is not for them to make the choice. Glory be to Allah. He is exalted far above their associating others in His Divinity.

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: And your Lord creates whatsoever He wills and chooses, no choice have they. Glorified is Allah, and exalted above all that they associate.

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: Thy Lord bringeth to pass what He willeth and chooseth. They have never any choice. Glorified be Allah and Exalted above all that they associate (with Him)!

68.author: Qarai
68.text: Your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses: they have no choice. Immaculate is Allah and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him].

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: Your Lord creates whosoever He will and He chooses the choice was not theirs (the unbelievers). Exalted is Allah, above that they associate!

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: And your Lord creates what He wills and chooses; not for them was the choice. Exalted is Allah and high above what they associate with Him.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: Your Lord creates and chooses (to grant mercy) to whomever He wants. (In matters of guidance) they (unbelievers) do not have the choice to choose whatever they want. God is too exalted to be considered equal to anything else.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: And your Lord creates and chooses whom He pleases; to choose is not theirs; glory be to Allah, and exalted be He above what they associate (with Him).

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: Your Lord creates whatsoever He wills and chooses whomsoever He pleases. They have no choice. Praise be to God -- exalted is He over anything they may associate with Him!

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: Thy Lord does create and choose as He pleases: no choice have they (in the matter): Glory to Allah! and far is He above the partners they ascribe (to Him)!

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Warabbuka yakhluqu ma yashao wayakhtaru ma kana lahumu alkhiyaratu subhana Allahi wataAAala AAamma yushrikoona

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: Your Lord well knows what they hide in their breasts and what they disclose.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: And your Lord knows what is hidden in their breasts, and what they disclose.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: And thy Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they publish.

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: And thy Lord knoweth that which their breasts conceal and that which they make known.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal, and what they reveal.

69.author: Itani
69.text: And your Lord knows what their hearts conceal, and what they reveal.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: Your Lord knows all that their hearts conceal and all that they reveal.

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal, and what they reveal.

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: And thy Lord knoweth what their breasts conceal, and what they publish.

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Your Lord knows whatever their breasts conceal, and whatever they disclose.

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: Your Lord knows what their chests hide and what they reveal.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Your Lord knows all that their hearts hide or reveal.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: And your Lord knows what their breasts conceal and what they manifest.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Your Lord knows what they conceal in their hearts and what they disclose.

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: And thy Lord knows all that their hearts conceal and all that they reveal.

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Warabbuka yaAAlamu ma tukinnu sudooruhum wama yuAAlinoona

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: He is God. There is no god but He. His alone is praise first and last, and His the judgement, and to Him will you be brought back in the end.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: And He is Allah – there is no God except Him; only His is the praise in this world and in the Hereafter; and only His is the command, and it is towards Him that you will be returned.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: And He is God; there is no god but He. His is the praise in the former as in the latter; His too is the Judgment, and unto Him you shall be returned.

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: And He is Allah there is no god but he! His is all praise in the first and in the last, and His is the command, and unto Him ye shall be returned.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: And He is Allah; La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). His is all praise, in the first (i.e. in this world) and in the last (i.e. in the Hereafter). And for Him is the Decision, and to Him shall you (all) be returned.

70.author: Itani
70.text: And He is God. There is no god but He. To Him belongs all praise in this life, and in the next. And His is the decision, and to Him you will be returned.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: He is Allah; there is no god but He. His is the praise in this world and in the Hereafter. His is the command and to Him will all of you be returned.

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: And He is Allah; La ilaha illa Huwa, His is the praise in the first and in the last, and His is the decision, and to Him shall you be returned.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: And He is Allah; there is no Allah save Him. His is all praise in the former and the latter (state), and His is the command, and unto Him ye will be brought back.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: He is Allah, there is no god except Him. All praise belongs to Him in this world and the Hereafter. All judgement belongs to Him, and to Him you will be brought back.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: And He is Allah; there is no god except He. The Praise is His in the former as in the latter. His is the Judgement, to Him you shall be returned.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: And He is Allah; there is no deity except Him. To Him is [due all] praise in the first [life] and the Hereafter. And His is the [final] decision, and to Him you will be returned.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: He is the only God and it is only He who deserves to be given thanks in this world and in the life to come. Judgment is in His hands and to Him you will all return.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: And He is Allah, there is no god but He! All praise is due to Him in this (life) and the hereafter, and His is the judgment, and to Him you shall be brought back.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: He is God: there is no god but Him. All Praise is due to Him in this world and the hereafter. His is the Judgement and to Him you shall be returned.

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: And He is Allah: There is no god but He. To Him be praise, at the first and at the last: for Him is the Command, and to Him shall ye (all) be brought back.

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Wahuwa Allahu la ilaha illa huwa lahu alhamdu fee aloola waalakhirati walahu alhukmu wailayhi turjaAAoona

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: Say: "Just think, if God were to cover you up with night for ever until the Day of Doom, what other god apart from God will give you light? Why do you not pay heed?"

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: Proclaim, “What is your opinion – if Allah makes it night continuously for you till the Day of Resurrection then, other than Allah, who is the God who could bring you light? So do you not heed?”

71.author: Arberry
71.text: Say: 'What think you? If God should make the night unceasing over you, until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God shall bring you illumination? Will you not hear?'

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: Say thou: bethink ye, if Allah made night continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, what god is there beside Allah, who would bring you light? Hearken ye not?

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Tell me! If Allah made night continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, who is an ilah (a god) besides Allah who could bring you light? Will you not then hear?"

71.author: Itani
71.text: Say, “Have you considered? Had God made the night perpetual over you until the Day of Resurrection, which god other than God will bring you illumination? Do you not hear?”

71.author: Maududi
71.text: (O Prophet), tell them: "Did you consider: if Allah were to make the night perpetual over you till the Day of Judgement, is there a god other than Allah who can bring forth light for you? Do you not hear?"

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: Say: "Tell me! If Allah made the night continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, which god besides Allah could bring you light Will you not then hear"

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: Say: Have ye thought, if Allah made night everlasting for you till the Day of Resurrection, who is a god beside Allah who could bring you light? Will ye not then hear?

71.author: Qarai
71.text: Say, ‘Tell me, if Allah were to make the night perpetual for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than Allah can bring you light? Will you not listen?’

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: Say: 'Think! What if Allah should enshroud you in unceasing night till the Day of Resurrection, what god, other than Allah, shall bring you light! Will you not hear?'

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: Say, "Have you considered: if Allah should make for you the night continuous until the Day of Resurrection, what deity other than Allah could bring you light? Then will you not hear?"

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Think, if God were to cause the night to continue until the Day of Judgment which Lord besides Him could bring you light? Will you then not listen to (His revelations)?"

71.author: Shakir
71.text: Say: Tell me, if Allah were to make the night to continue incessantly on you till the day of resurrection, who is the god besides Allah that could bring you light? Do you not then hear?

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: Ask them, "Tell me, if God were to extend perpetual night over you till the Day of Judgement, is there any deity other than God that could bring you light? Will you not listen?"

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: Say: See ye? If Allah were to make the night perpetual over you to the Day of Judgment, what god is there other than Allah, who can give you enlightenment? Will ye not then hearken?

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Qul araaytum in jaAAala Allahu AAalaykumu allayla sarmadan ila yawmi alqiyamati man ilahun ghayru Allahi yateekum bidiyain afala tasmaAAoona

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: Say: "Just think. If God were to make the day perpetual till the Day of Resurrection, what other god but God would bring you night for rest? Why do you not reflect?

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: Say, “What is your opinion – if Allah makes it day continuously for you till the Day of Resurrection then, other than Allah, who is the God who could bring you night for you to rest during it? So do you not perceive?”

72.author: Arberry
72.text: Say: 'What think you? If God should make the day unceasing over you, until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than God shall bring you night to repose in? Will you not see?

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Say thou: bethink ye, if Allah made day continuous for you till the Day of Resurrection, what god is there beside Allah, who would bring you night wherein ye have repose? Behold ye not?

72.author: Hilali & Khan
